title: CloudDash API Rate Limits
content:
CloudDash enforces rate limits on API requests to ensure platform stability and fair usage across all customers. Limits vary by plan tier and are applied per organization per API key.

Starter Plan Limits: 100 requests per minute for read endpoints (GET), 20 requests per minute for write endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE). Burst allowance of 150 read requests for short spikes. Exceeding limits returns HTTP 429 with headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset (Unix timestamp), and Retry-After (seconds until reset).

Pro Plan Limits: 1,000 requests per minute for read endpoints, 200 requests per minute for write endpoints. Burst allowance of 1,500 read requests. Suitable for most CI/CD pipelines and automated dashboard management.

Enterprise Plan Limits: 10,000 requests per minute for read endpoints, 2,000 for write endpoints. Custom limits available upon request for high-volume metric ingestion via the Metrics Ingest API (up to 100,000 data points per minute).

Rate Limit Scope: Limits apply per API key, not per user. Organizations using multiple keys share the organization's aggregate throughput ceiling (2x single-key limit for Pro, 5x for Enterprise). Internal CloudDash operations (dashboard auto-refresh, alert evaluation) do not count against your API limits.

Handling 429 Responses: Implement exponential backoff in your API clients. Read the Retry-After header and wait before retrying. Our official SDKs (Python, Node.js, Go) handle retries automatically with configurable max attempts.

Metrics Ingest API: Separate limits apply to POST /v1/metrics/ingest: Starter 1,000 points/minute, Pro 50,000 points/minute, Enterprise 500,000 points/minute. Each data point counts as one unit regardless of payload size.

Monitoring Usage: View current API usage under Settings > API & Integrations > Usage Dashboard. Enterprise customers receive weekly usage reports and alerts when consumption exceeds 80% of limits.
